methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $626M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: Nominal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Poolside is a Series B AI & Software company founded by Jason Warner and Eiso Kant that has raised $626M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage peers including Figure AI ($39.0B Series C), Skild AI ($14.0B Series C), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B Series B), and Nominal ($1.0B Series B), supplemented by public reference points including Palantir.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $626M raised as a proxy baseline, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $1.2B. Peer-median analysis across the four comps produces a $9.8B valuation, with a range spanning $1.0B to $39.0B. The stage-progression method is mechanically anchored to capital raised; the peer method captures actual transaction evidence but reflects heterogeneous staging and market timing across the cohort.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.0B (low) to $11.3B (high), with a median of $5.5B. The wide dispersion—reflecting both the substantial valuation variance within early-stage AI (Nominal at $1.0B versus Figure at $39.0B) and Poolside's Series B positioning relative to adjacent-stage peers—indicates material uncertainty around terminal value. Key sensitivities include stage-specific capital deployment, revenue traction relative to peers, and duration to next funding event.
